story_shape: the man protecting Arch Manning's blindside -- a first-team All-SEC tackle back for the quarterback's defining year

plan.spine: a first-team All-SEC left tackle returns to protect Arch Manning's blindside in Manning's draft-year season
blend: 2025: first-team All-SEC in his first full year starting
allowed just 3 sacks and 3 QB hurries protecting Arch Manning
the returning blindside protector on a national-title contender
declined a favorable draft projection to come back
plan.payoff: first-team All-SEC and a near-clean sheet at left tackle
plan.forward_stake: whether continuity at left tackle helps make Manning's year the one Texas needs

hook: If Arch Manning is going to have the year Texas needs, someone has to keep him clean. Trevor Goosby did it as a first-team All-SEC tackle in 2025 -- and he came back to do it again.
expand: In his first full year as a starter, Goosby was first-team All-SEC and a wall at left tackle -- just three sacks and three quarterback hurries allowed protecting Arch Manning's blindside. He declined a favorable draft projection to return, giving Texas the most valuable kind of continuity for a title run: a proven blindside protector entering the most scrutinized quarterback season in the country. The forward stakes are tied directly to Manning's: a clean pocket is the difference between a Heisman campaign and a frustrated one, and Goosby is the reason it stays clean. He's not the headline name on the roster -- he's the one who makes the headline name possible.
style: Goosby is a pass-protection-first left tackle whose value shows up in what doesn't happen -- the sacks that never reach the quarterback. The 2025 ledger is the case: three sacks and three hurries allowed across a full SEC season protecting a marquee quarterback, the kind of line that earns first-team all-conference. He has the length and footwork to handle SEC edge rushers and the anchor to hold up against the bull rush. He's a steady, technically sound blindside protector rather than a road-grading mauler -- and on a team whose season rides on its quarterback staying upright, that's the most important job on the line.
style_layers_pending: no PBP for an offensive lineman -- sacks/hurries-allowed + All-SEC honor + attributed scouting grounded; fan-vs-rival discourse descriptors pending discourse-extraction cleanup
